opendb_id.description: Unique identifier for the document (UUID v4)

color.description: The color(s) of this product. Each color of the product should be included in the array. The color of the fan frame and blades.

quantity.description:
Number of separately mountable fan units included in the package. Use the total number of fans only for multi-packs of separate fans.

Single-frame multi-fan products count as 1 unit. Example: an NZXT F360-style 360mm single-frame triple-fan unit should use quantity 1, not 3.

min_airflow.description: The minimum airflow of the fan in CFM. If it is a constant, just fill in min_airflow and ignore max_airflow

max_airflow.description: The maximum airflow of the fan in CFM. If it is a constant, just fill in min_airflow and ignore max_airflow

min_noise_level.description: The minimum noise level of the fan in dB. If it is a constant, just fill in min_noise_level and ignore max_noise_level

max_noise_level.description: The maximum noise level of the fan in dB. If it is a constant, just fill in min_noise_level and ignore max_noise_level

pwm.description: Whether the fan supports Pulse Width Modulation for speed control

led.description: The type of LED lighting, if any (e.g., 'Addressable RGB', 'RGB', 'None')

connector.description: The type of connector(s) the fan uses (e.g., '4-pin PWM + 3-pin 5V Addressable RGB')

controller.description: If the fan includes a controller, specify the type (e.g., '5V Addressable RGB'). Otherwise, indicate 'None'

static_pressure.description: The static pressure of the fan in mmH₂O

isOEM.description: Whether this fan is OEM-only (bundled with cases, not sold separately)

general_product_information.description: General product information and retailer SKUs

manufacturer.description: The company that produced the case fans.

series.description:
Product series name

The series is the manufacturer's marketed product line / family for the fan. Manufacturer (e.g., Noctua, Arctic, Lian Li, Corsair, be quiet!, Thermalright, Phanteks) is stored separately. Fan size (`size`: 120, 140, 200), pack quantity (`quantity`), color (`manufacturer_color`), PWM (`pwm`), LED (`led`), and connector are also their own fields and MUST NOT be in the series. Do NOT include pack size like '3 Pack' / '3-Pack' in the series — that is captured by `quantity`. The series name often encodes the size (e.g., 'NF-A12x25' → 120mm, 'NF-A14' → 140mm) because the manufacturer markets the same family with different SKUs per size; that is fine — keep the size in the series name when the manufacturer's SKU does. Examples: 'Noctua NF-A12x25 PWM 120mm' → series='NF-A12x25'. 'Noctua NF-A14 PWM 140mm' → series='NF-A14'. 'Noctua NF-A12x25 chromax.black.swap' → series='NF-A12x25'. 'Arctic P12 PWM PST 120mm 5-Pack' → series='P12'. 'Arctic P12 Max 120mm' → series='P12 Max' (Max is a distinct product line, not a variant of P12). 'Arctic BioniX P120 120mm' → series='BioniX P120'. 'Lian Li UNI Fan SL120 V2 120mm 3-Pack' → series='UNI Fan SL'. 'Lian Li UNI Fan TL120 LCD' → series='UNI Fan TL LCD'. 'Corsair iCUE LINK QX120 RGB 120mm Triple Pack' → series='iCUE LINK QX120'. 'Corsair LL120 RGB 120mm 3-Pack' → series='LL120'. 'be quiet! Silent Wings 4 PWM 120mm' → series='Silent Wings 4'. 'be quiet! Light Wings 120mm PWM ARGB 3-Pack' → series='Light Wings'. 'Thermalright TL-C12C-S 120mm 3-Pack' → series='TL-C12C'. The series is what stays consistent across pack-size / color / PWM SKUs of the same product line.

variant.description:
Specific variant in the series

Most fans should have an empty variant. Pack quantity, fan size, color, PWM, LED, connector, and flow direction are all stored in their own fields. Only fill `variant` when the manufacturer ships a genuinely distinct sub-model under the same series name that needs its own selector and is NOT already captured by another field. Examples that should be EMPTY: 'Noctua NF-A12x25 PWM 120mm' → variant='' (PWM is in the `pwm` field). 'Noctua NF-A12x25 chromax.black.swap' → variant='chromax.black.swap' ONLY IF chromax.black.swap is treated as a distinct sub-model — many catalogs put 'chromax.black.swap' in `manufacturer_color` instead. Pick one approach and be consistent across the catalog. 'Arctic P12 PWM PST 120mm 5-Pack' → variant='PST' if you want to distinguish PWM-only from PWM+PST SKUs (PST = PWM Sharing Technology, not captured by `pwm`); otherwise variant=''. 'Lian Li UNI Fan SL120 V2 3-Pack' → variant='V2' (V2 is a marketed hardware revision and the V1 SKU is still sold). 'Lian Li UNI Fan SL120 V2 Reverse' → variant='V2' with `flow_direction='Reverse'` (do not duplicate Reverse in the variant). 'Corsair iCUE LINK QX120 RGB Starter Kit (Triple Pack with iCUE LINK System Hub)' → variant='Starter Kit' if the bundle SKU is sold separately. The variant must be unique within a (manufacturer, series, size, manufacturer_color, quantity) group; if every fan in a group ends up with the same value, leave it empty.

manufacturer_color.description:
Manufacturer's official color name for this product variant.

Use the manufacturer's marketed colorway / frame finish name verbatim. Common fan colorways: 'Black', 'White', 'Brown / Beige' (Noctua's classic look), 'chromax.black' (Noctua blacked-out frame and blades), 'chromax.black.swap' (chromax.black with swappable colored anti-vibration pads — many catalogs use this string here instead of in `variant`; pick one consistently), 'Phantom Black'. For multi-tone fans (e.g., a black frame with white blades), separate tones with ' / ' (e.g., 'Black / White'). Branded special / collab colorways DO belong here when the manufacturer markets them as a distinct colorway and the rest of the SKU is identical: 'Hatsune Miku Edition', 'Halo Edition'. Most stock fans are 'Black'. Do NOT put fan size ('120mm', '140mm'), pack count ('3-Pack'), 'PWM', 'PST', 'RGB', 'ARGB', or family / model words like 'NF-A12x25', 'P12', 'UNI Fan' in manufacturer_color — those belong in dedicated fields or the series. Color is purely the visual finish of the fan frame and blades.

last_manually_spec_verified_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the part specs were last manually verified by a human
